mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS level reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, which supports S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when policy is already near the effective lower bound and inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if lower FEDFUNDS does not coincide with higher 20d SPX returns in walk-forward testing, or if the signal's rank correlation stays below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recession-like threshold signals deteriorating household income and weaker earnings expectations, which pushes S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 7.0% or increases by at least 0.3pp from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the signal flips positive in any 2-year rolling validation window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate, the economy is generating stronger nominal sales and payroll income simultaneously, supporting earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. Either series alone is insufficient; the joint confirmation matters.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-PAYEMS acceleration indicator does not outperform each single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if positive 60d returns do not exceed 55% when both series improve together.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO indicates higher factory output and tighter real activity momentum, which supports cyclical earnings and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect weakens if output is rising only because of a temporary post-shock rebound.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO gains are not followed by higher-than-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the signal has negative cumulative alpha in any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals weaker housing demand, softer construction activity, and slower cyclical growth, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is most informative when housing starts fall below their recent trend rather than when they remain stable.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if large negative HOUST surprises are followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the trigger loses directional accuracy below 45% in any rolling 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is a tightening shock: inflation pressures force policy restraint, raise real rates, and compress equity multiples. Under this condition, S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS over the last 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-policy tightening condition fails to produce negative 20d returns, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS alongside a stable or rising UNRATE identifies job-market deterioration that should reduce consumer spending expectations and pressure S&P 500 returns negative over the next quarter. The labor signal is strongest when payroll losses are broad rather than one-month noise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for two consecutive months while UNRATE is unchanged or higher
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-unemployment deterioration does not precede below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the signal is not negative in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 after a prior contraction marks a post-downturn demand recovery that supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when GDP growth is offset by weakening retail sales and labor conditions.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises from the prior quarter after a negative quarter
failure_condition: Reject if post-contraction GDP rebounds do not generate positive 60d SPX returns on average, or if the effect disappears when RSAFS and PAYEMS fail to confirm the GDP recovery.
